theory.intro: A polygon is regular if all its sides are equal AND all its angles are equal. If either varies, it is irregular.
facts: **Regular**: all sides equal and all angles equal (e.g. square, equilateral triangle).
**Irregular**: at least one side or angle is different.
Equal sides are shown by tick marks; equal angles are often shown by matching arc marks.
steps: Check whether all sides are equal (look at the tick marks or lengths).
Check whether all angles are equal.
If both are equal it is regular; otherwise it is irregular.
